title: Croatian to B1 — the validated path

days.title: A2 kickoff: Zemlje i jezici
days.objective: Start the A2 e-course and talk about countries, nationalities and languages.
days.paretoFocus: The free University of Zagreb A2 course is 80 units of exactly the right material. One unit a day, every day, is the whole engine of this phase — start the habit today.
drills: Sign in at a2.ffzg.unizg.hr (same free login as A1) and do Unit 1 of A2.hr.
Learn 12 country-language pairs: Njemačka/njemački, Francuska/francuski, Španjolska/španjolski…
Say which languages you speak, understand and are learning.
Skim the 8-module map of the course so you know the road ahead.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall 8 countries with their languages.
Say 'Učim hrvatski jer…' with your real reason.

days.title: Language adjectives: -ski, -ški, -čki
days.objective: Form relational adjectives (hrvatski, praški, grčki) from place names.
days.paretoFocus: One suffix family turns every country or city you know into an adjective — instant vocabulary multiplication.
drills: Do Unit 2 of A2.hr.
Form 10 adjectives: hrvatski, engleski, njemački, zagrebački, splitski…
Note languages are lowercase in Croatian: govorim hrvatski.
Describe 5 things by origin: talijanska kava, francuski film.

items: Turn 6 place names into adjectives from memory.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review yesterday's country-language pairs.

days.title: Possessive adjectives: bratov, sestrin
days.objective: Say whose something is with -ov/-ev/-in adjectives.
days.paretoFocus: Croatian prefers 'bratov auto' over 'auto od brata' — mastering this small pattern makes your possession sound native.
drills: Do Unit 3 of A2.hr.
Form possessives: brat → bratov, muž → mužev, sestra → sestrin.
Describe 6 objects around you by owner (Ovo je ženina knjiga.).
Contrast animate vs inanimate possessives.

items: Make possessive adjectives from 5 names.
Review -ski adjectives.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.

days.title: Accusative across the whole phrase
days.objective: Decline adjectives, demonstratives and possessives in the accusative, not just nouns.
days.paretoFocus: A1 gave you noun endings; A2 makes the whole phrase agree ('Vidim ovog novog kolegu'). This is the single biggest step toward correct Croatian.
drills: Do Unit 4 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · adjective declension (accusative).
Decline 6 full phrases: taj dobar čovjek → tog dobrog čovjeka.
Write 5 'I see/know/love + full phrase' sentences.

items: Say 'I see this new colleague' with full agreement.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall the animate vs inanimate accusative rule.

days.title: svoj — 'one's own'
days.objective: Use the reflexive-possessive svoj when the owner is the subject.
days.paretoFocus: 'Volim svoj posao' vs 'Volim njegov posao' changes the meaning entirely. svoj is obligatory in Croatian and has no English equivalent — drill it early.
drills: Do Unit 5 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · reflexive svoj.
Contrast pairs: Čitam svoju knjigu / Čitam njezinu knjigu.
Fix 5 sentences where moj/tvoj should be svoj.

items: Explain in one sentence when svoj is used.
Review accusative phrase agreement.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.

days.title: Output day: Moji jezici
days.objective: Speak and write about your language history and goals.
days.paretoFocus: Producing a personal text locks in the week's grammar with content you'll reuse in every future conversation about learning Croatian.
drills: Do Unit 6 of A2.hr.
Write 8 sentences: which languages you speak, since when, and why Croatian.
Deliver it aloud twice as a 90-second talk.
Slip in one -ski adjective, one possessive and one svoj.

items: Re-deliver your talk without notes.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 3 words you were missing and add them.

days.title: Week 9 review + speaking
days.objective: Consolidate the first six units in live conversation.
days.paretoFocus: A weekly speaking session converts a week of study into skill — and if you live with a Croatian speaker, 15 minutes of chat with your spouse counts fully as tutor time.
drills: Have a 15-minute conversation about countries, languages and learning (tutor or your Croatian spouse).
Re-do the exercises of your weakest A2.hr unit this week.
If any A1.hr units are still unfinished, clear one now.
Note 5 corrections from the conversation.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say the 5 corrected sentences.
Recall: -ski adjectives, possessives, svoj — one example each.

days.title: Kad će taj vikend? — making plans
days.objective: Talk about upcoming weekend plans with futur I and time phrases.
days.paretoFocus: Weekend plans are the small talk you'll actually have every Friday; the theme recycles futur I while new grammar loads underneath.
drills: Do Unit 7 of A2.hr.
Learn plan vocabulary: izlazak, roštilj, izlet, odmor, druženje.
Make 6 futur I sentences about this weekend (Ići ću…, Naći ćemo se…).
Ask someone 'Što radiš u subotu?' and answer for yourself.

items: Say three real plans for this weekend.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall the short forms ću/ćeš/će.

days.title: Pronouns as objects: me, te, ga, je
days.objective: Use accusative personal pronouns in stressed and unstressed forms.
days.paretoFocus: 'Vidiš li me? — Vidim te.' Object pronouns appear in almost every exchange; the short (clitic) forms are what natives use 95% of the time.
drills: Do Unit 8 of A2.hr.
Learn the pairs: mene/me, tebe/te, njega/ga, nju/ju (je), nas, vas, njih/ih.
Answer 6 questions replacing the noun with a pronoun (Vidiš li Marka? — Vidim ga.).
Note stressed forms for emphasis and after prepositions (za mene).

items: Replace the object with a pronoun in 4 sentences.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review weekend-plan phrases.

days.title: Clitic order — the sentence skeleton
days.objective: Place enclitics in the fixed order: li → auxiliary → dative → accusative → se → je.
days.paretoFocus: Clitic order is THE grammar that separates A2 from A1 speech. One rule ('second position, fixed internal order') fixes hundreds of future sentences.
drills: Do Unit 9 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · clitic order (redoslijed enklitika).
Build: Jučer sam mu ga dao. / Dala mi je knjigu.
Reorder 6 scrambled sentences into correct clitic order.

items: Recite the clitic order chain from memory.
Say 'I gave it to him yesterday.'
Clear your due words in the Words tab.

days.title: New present class: krenem, dođem
days.objective: Conjugate -(n)uti verbs (krenuti → krenem) and doći → dođem.
days.paretoFocus: This class covers key motion and event verbs (krenuti, stignuti, doći). With Unit 10 done you finish Module 1 of 8 — the system is working.
drills: Do Unit 10 of A2.hr — Module 1 complete!
Study Grammar tab: A2 · more present-tense classes.
Conjugate krenuti and doći in full.
Make 5 sentences about arriving and setting off this weekend.

items: Conjugate doći cold.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review clitic order with one dative+accusative sentence.

days.title: Invitations & arrangements
days.objective: Invite, accept and politely decline in Croatian.
days.paretoFocus: Arranging to meet is a core A2 can-do; a handful of fixed patterns (Dođi k nama! / Može! / Žao mi je, ne mogu…) covers the whole social ritual.
drills: Do Unit 11 of A2.hr.
Learn: Dođi k nama u subotu! / Hoćemo li se naći…? / Odgovara li ti…?
Role-play one invitation accepted and one declined with a reason.
Practise pronoun clitics inside the invitations (Vidimo se! Javi mi se!).

items: Invite someone to coffee from memory.
Decline politely with 'jer' + reason.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.

days.title: Write it: a weekend-plan message
days.objective: Write a natural 8-10 sentence message arranging the weekend.
days.paretoFocus: Writing forces you to place every clitic and pronoun deliberately — and a native correction from your partner is free tutoring.
drills: Do Unit 12 of A2.hr.
Write a message to a friend proposing a weekend plan (time, place, activity).
Check every enclitic sits in second position.
Read it to your partner and ask for corrections.

items: Read your message aloud for flow.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review invitation phrases.

days.title: Week 10 review + speaking: plan a real weekend
days.objective: Negotiate real weekend plans entirely in Croatian.
days.paretoFocus: Using this week's clitics and pronouns to plan something that actually happens is the strongest possible memory glue.
drills: 15-minute conversation: plan the coming weekend in Croatian (tutor, partner or spouse).
Re-do the trickiest exercise from Units 7-12.
Apply any corrections you received on your posted message.
Record a 60-second summary of the agreed plan.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say 3 sentences with perfect clitic order.
Recall the accusative pronoun pairs (mene/me…).

days.title: Kvaliteta života — how you live
days.objective: Describe your home, neighbourhood and daily habits.
days.paretoFocus: Quality-of-life vocabulary (stan, kvart, navike, slobodno vrijeme) powers the conversations natives most like to have — comparing how people live.
drills: Do Unit 13 of A2.hr.
Learn 15 home-and-lifestyle words: stan, kvart, susjed, navika, odmor, buka, mir…
Describe your home in 5 sentences.
Say one habit you love and one you want to change.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Describe your neighbourhood in 3 sentences.
Review weekend-plan vocabulary.

days.title: Locative across the whole phrase
days.objective: Decline adjectives, demonstratives and possessives in the locative.
days.paretoFocus: 'U ovom malom stanu', 'u svojoj sobi' — full locative phrases let you say precisely where things happen, the backbone of describing your life.
drills: Do Unit 14 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · adjective declension (locative).
Decline 6 phrases: taj veliki grad → u tom velikom gradu.
Write 5 sentences about where you live, work and relax.

items: Say 'in my own room' (u svojoj sobi).
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Contrast accusative (motion) vs locative (location) in one pair.

days.title: Talking about where you live
days.objective: Give a rich, connected description of your living situation.
days.paretoFocus: This is the A2 can-do 'describing home and surroundings' — and it forces yesterday's locative phrases into real use.
drills: Do Unit 15 of A2.hr.
Describe your flat room by room, with adjectives agreeing in the locative.
Compare where you live now with where you grew up (2-3 sentences).
Ask and answer: U kakvom stanu živiš? Što ti se sviđa u svom kvartu?

items: Deliver a 60-second home tour aloud.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review locative phrase endings.

days.title: Verb aspect — one idea, two verbs
days.objective: Understand the perfective/imperfective pair system properly.
days.paretoFocus: Aspect is the heart of the Slavic verb. Grasping that pisati/napisati are one meaning with two viewpoints unlocks how natives think about every action.
drills: Do Unit 16 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · verbal aspect (glagolski vid).
Sort 10 verbs into imperfective/perfective: pisati/napisati, kupovati/kupiti, piti/popiti…
For 5 daily actions, decide: process or completed result?

items: Explain aspect in one sentence, out loud.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Give both members of 5 aspect pairs.

days.title: Aspect in the past
days.objective: Choose the right aspect when narrating: background vs event.
days.paretoFocus: 'Dok sam pisao pismo, nazvala me sestra.' Imperfective paints the scene, perfective moves the story — the rule behind every anecdote you'll ever tell.
drills: Do Unit 17 of A2.hr.
Rewrite 5 past sentences switching aspect and observe the meaning change.
Tell a 4-sentence story: background imperfective, events perfective.
Underline every verb in the story and justify its aspect.

items: Re-tell your mini-story from memory.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall 5 aspect pairs cold.

days.title: Output: my quality of life
days.objective: Speak for two minutes about how you live and how you'd like to live.
days.paretoFocus: Combining home vocabulary, locative phrases and past-tense aspect in one talk is exactly the integrated performance A2 demands.
drills: Do Unit 18 of A2.hr.
Prepare and record a 2-minute talk: your home, your habits, what you'd change.
Read the matching Kvaliteta života texts in Razgovarajte s nama! if you have it.
Listen back and note 3 errors to fix.

items: Re-record the weakest 30 seconds.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review aspect-in-the-past with one example.

days.title: Week 11 review + speaking
days.objective: Consolidate locative phrases and aspect in conversation.
days.paretoFocus: Aspect only becomes automatic through live use — a conversation about daily life forces dozens of aspect decisions in real time.
drills: 15-minute conversation about homes, habits and daily life.
Ask your partner to flag every aspect mistake.
Re-do the hardest exercise from Units 13-18.
Note your personal top-3 aspect confusions.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say one background+event story sentence perfectly.
Review locative phrase agreement.

days.title: Aspect with future and modals
days.objective: Pick the right aspect after ću/moram/želim.
days.paretoFocus: 'Sutra ću napisati izvještaj' (finish it) vs 'Sutra ću pisati' (spend time on it) — aspect after modals and futur I is where learners most often sound off.
drills: Do Unit 19 of A2.hr.
Make 6 future sentences, three per aspect, and explain each choice.
Combine modals with the right aspect: Moram kupiti… / Volim kuhati…
Spot-fix 4 sentences with the wrong aspect.

items: Say 'Tomorrow I'll write (and finish) the email.'
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall your top-3 aspect confusions from yesterday.

days.title: Fifteen aspect pairs you'll actually use
days.objective: Memorise the highest-frequency aspect pairs as single vocabulary items.
days.paretoFocus: Learning pairs together (davati/dati, počinjati/početi, viđati/vidjeti) means you never have to derive them again. Unit 20 closes Module 2 — a quarter done!
drills: Do Unit 20 of A2.hr — Module 2 complete!
Drill 15 core pairs both directions (English → both verbs).
Write 5 sentence pairs showing the same action in both aspects.
Add the pairs to your Words tab queue.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite 10 pairs cold.
Review aspect after modals with one example.

days.title: Planovi, želje, mogućnosti
days.objective: Talk about plans, wishes and possibilities with verb + infinitive patterns.
days.paretoFocus: planirati/željeti/namjeravati/moći + infinitive is a tiny pattern with huge range — everything you intend, hope or can do fits in it.
drills: Do Unit 21 of A2.hr.
Learn: planirati, namjeravati, željeti, nadati se, uspjeti.
State 3 plans, 3 wishes and 3 possibilities for this year.
Mind the aspect of each infinitive you choose.

items: Say one plan, one wish, one possibility.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review 5 aspect pairs.

days.title: The conditional: bih, bi, bismo
days.objective: Form kondicional I with and without the pronoun.
days.paretoFocus: The conditional is how Croatian does politeness, wishes and hypotheticals — three conversational superpowers from one small paradigm.
drills: Do Unit 22 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · the conditional (kondicional prvi).
Learn bih, bi, bi, bismo, biste, bi + participle: Rado bih putovao.
Transform 6 present sentences into conditional wishes.

items: Conjugate the conditional of ići cold.
Say 'I would gladly travel.'
Clear your due words in the Words tab.

days.title: Polite Croatian: Biste li…?
days.objective: Use negative and question forms of the conditional for polite requests.
days.paretoFocus: 'Biste li mogli…?' and 'Ne bih htio smetati…' are the politeness formulas of adult Croatian — instantly upgrading how you sound in shops, offices and emails.
drills: Do Unit 23 of A2.hr.
Form questions: Biste li mi pomogli? Bi li došla?
Form negatives: Ne bih rekao. Ne bismo mogli.
Role-play 3 polite requests: at the post office, to a neighbour, in an email.

items: Make a polite request from memory.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall where the clitic 'bih' sits in the sentence.

days.title: Wishes and unreal conditions
days.objective: Express wishes, possibilities and unreal conditions with kad + conditional.
days.paretoFocus: 'Kad bih imao više vremena, putovao bih.' The four meanings of the conditional (wish, possibility, condition, impossible condition) cover most of what 'would' does in English.
drills: Do Unit 24 of A2.hr.
Build 4 sentences, one per conditional meaning: želja, mogućnost, uvjet, nemogući uvjet.
Answer: Što bi radio da ne moraš raditi?
Translate 4 English 'would' sentences into Croatian.

items: Say a 'kad bih… , … bih' sentence cold.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review polite Biste li…? requests.

days.title: Week 12 review + speaking: dreams & plans
days.objective: Hold a conversation about plans and dreams driven by the conditional.
days.paretoFocus: Talking about what you would do is the most natural possible drill for this week's grammar — and among the most engaging conversations to have.
drills: 15-minute conversation: plans, wishes and what-ifs (tutor or conversation partner).
Use at least 5 conditionals and 3 different aspect pairs.
Re-do the hardest exercise from Units 19-24.
Note recurring errors for next week's focus.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say your best 'kad bih…' sentence.
Recite the conditional paradigm once.

days.title: Dative across the whole phrase
days.objective: Decline adjectives, demonstratives and possessives in the dative.
days.paretoFocus: 'Pomažem svojoj mlađoj sestri' — full dative phrases complete the recipient side of your sentences and mirror the locative forms you already know.
drills: Do Unit 25 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · adjective declension (dative).
Decline 6 phrases: moja dobra prijateljica → mojoj dobroj prijateljici.
Write 5 giving/helping/writing-to sentences with full phrases.

items: Say 'to my younger sister' with full agreement.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note: dative and locative adjective endings match.

days.title: Dative verbs & svoj in the dative
days.objective: Use the common dative-governing verbs with correct phrases.
days.paretoFocus: A short verb list (pomoći, javiti se, vjerovati, sviđati se, čestitati) triggers the dative constantly — knowing the list means never guessing the case.
drills: Do Unit 26 of A2.hr.
Learn 8 dative verbs and make one sentence with each.
Practise sviđati se with clitics: Sviđa mi se tvoj stan.
Use svoj in the dative: Javio sam se svojoj obitelji.

items: List 6 dative verbs from memory.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'I like your flat' the Croatian way (dative + sviđati se).

days.title: Što bi bilo kad bi… — hypothetical day
days.objective: Sustain hypothetical talk combining conditional, dative and aspect.
days.paretoFocus: Free hypothetical conversation is where the conditional stops being a paradigm and becomes an instinct.
drills: Do Unit 27 of A2.hr.
Answer aloud: Što bi radio kad bi dobio slobodnu godinu? Kome bi prvo javio dobre vijesti?
Write 5 chained hypotheticals (Kad bih…, … bih, a onda bih…).
Open the Grammar tab → A2 · the conditional; read the examples aloud.

items: Deliver one 30-second hypothetical monologue.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review dative verbs.

days.title: Write it: Moji planovi i želje
days.objective: Write a 120-word text on your plans and wishes and get it corrected.
days.paretoFocus: This text is the week's grammar exam disguised as a diary entry — conditionals, datives and aspect all have to land correctly.
drills: Do Unit 28 of A2.hr.
Write ~120 words: real plans (futur I), wishes (conditional), people involved (dative).
Self-check every conditional clitic and svoj.
Read it to your partner for native corrections.

items: Read your text aloud for flow.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 3 constructions you weren't sure about.

days.title: Transport i komunikacija — getting around
days.objective: Command the vocabulary of transport and travel logistics.
days.paretoFocus: vlak, tramvaj, presjedanje, red vožnje, kašnjenje — transport words plus your case system handle tickets, delays and directions anywhere in Croatia.
drills: Do Unit 29 of A2.hr.
Learn 15 transport words: kolodvor, peron, polazak, dolazak, presjedanje, karta u jednom smjeru…
Say how you get to work/town: instrumental of means (tramvajem, autom, pješice).
Ask 3 timetable questions: Kada polazi…? S kojeg perona…?

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Buy a return ticket to Rijeka in a role-play.
Say your commute in one sentence with instrumental.

days.title: Present class: pišem, kažem
days.objective: Conjugate the -ati → -jem class (pisati → pišem, kazati → kažem).
days.paretoFocus: This class hides some of the most frequent verbs in the language (pisati, kazati, plakati, slati → šaljem). Unit 30 closes Module 3.
drills: Do Unit 30 of A2.hr — Module 3 complete!
Study Grammar tab: A2 · more present-tense classes.
Conjugate pisati, kazati and slati in full.
Write 5 sentences about writing and sending messages.

items: Conjugate slati (šaljem…) cold.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review transport vocabulary.

days.title: Week 13 review + speaking
days.objective: Consolidate dative, conditional and transport talk in conversation.
days.paretoFocus: Three modules are done — a spoken checkpoint now catches any drift before the intensive middle stretch of the course.
drills: 15-minute conversation: how you get around + what you'd change about local transport.
Apply corrections from your posted plans-and-wishes text.
Re-do the weakest exercise from Units 25-30.
Record 60 seconds and listen for clitic-order slips.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say 3 corrected sentences from your text.
Recall 6 dative verbs and the conditional paradigm.

days.title: Tickets, timetables, announcements
days.objective: Understand spoken travel information: platforms, delays, announcements.
days.paretoFocus: Catching essential info from short public announcements is a named A2 can-do — and the difference between catching and missing your bus.
drills: Do Unit 31 of A2.hr.
Listen to the unit dialogues twice without reading, then once with the text.
Extract from each: destination, time, platform/stop, price.
Role-play buying a ticket with one complication (delay or transfer).

items: Say the numbers 100-1000 in tens, fast.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall 5 announcement words (polazak, kašnjenje…).

days.title: Staying in touch: zvati/nazvati, slati/poslati
days.objective: Talk about calls, messages and mail with the right aspect every time.
days.paretoFocus: Communication verbs come in aspect pairs you'll use daily — nazvati me / zvati me is exactly the distinction that makes your Croatian precise.
drills: Do Unit 32 of A2.hr.
Drill the pairs: zvati/nazvati, slati/poslati, javljati se/javiti se, odgovarati/odgovoriti.
Narrate yesterday's communications: who called, who wrote, who replied.
Combine with clitics: Poslao sam ti je jučer. (I sent it to you yesterday.)

items: Give both aspects of 4 communication verbs.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'Call me tomorrow!' and 'He was calling me all day.'

days.title: Directions in the city
days.objective: Give and follow multi-step directions.
days.paretoFocus: Directions chain imperatives, accusatives and locatives into the most practical navigation skill there is.
drills: Do Unit 33 of A2.hr.
Learn: skrenite lijevo/desno, idite ravno, preko puta, iza ugla, do semafora.
Explain the route from your home to the nearest shop, step by step.
Follow a spoken 4-step route from the unit and sketch it.

items: Give 3-step directions from memory.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review communication verb pairs.

days.title: Clitics under pressure
days.objective: Keep clitic order intact in questions and fast exchanges.
days.paretoFocus: 'Možeš li mi ga poslati?' — questions with li plus two pronoun clitics are where order breaks down in real speech. Drilling it now makes it unbreakable.
drills: Do Unit 34 of A2.hr.
Build 8 questions stacking li + dative + accusative clitics.
Answer each at speed, replacing nouns with pronouns.
Review Grammar tab: A2 · clitic order and self-test the chain.

items: Say 'Can you send it to me?' instantly.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite the clitic chain (li → aux → dat → acc → se → je).

days.title: Day 100: verbs of motion & aspect
days.objective: Use ići and its perfective family (otići, doći, stići, krenuti) accurately.
days.paretoFocus: One hundred days in, and the motion-verb family is the perfect emblem of your progress: one imperfective base, a fleet of precise perfectives.
drills: Do Unit 35 of A2.hr.
Map the family: ići (go) → otići (leave), doći (arrive), stići (make it), krenuti (set off).
Narrate a door-to-door trip using at least 4 of them.
Celebrate day 100 by listing 5 things you can say now that you couldn't on day 1.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Conjugate doći and krenuti in the present.
Re-tell your door-to-door trip cold.

days.title: Write it: a complaint or inquiry
days.objective: Write a short formal message about a transport or service problem.
days.paretoFocus: A polite complaint mixes formal register, conditionals and precise facts — real-world writing you'll genuinely need, and a perfect correction target.
drills: Do Unit 36 of A2.hr.
Write 8-10 sentences: a late bus, a lost package or a wrong charge (what, when, what you request).
Use vi-forms and one polite conditional (Molim Vas… / Biste li mogli…).
Read it to your partner and fix what they flag.

items: Read the message aloud in a formal tone.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review directions vocabulary.

days.title: Week 14 speaking day (+ Unit 37)
days.objective: Talk travel and transport live, then bank one lighter unit.
days.paretoFocus: Speaking weeks keep pace with study weeks. A travel conversation with your tutor — or your Croatian spouse over coffee, which counts just the same — cements the whole module.
drills: 15-minute conversation: journeys, transport gripes and travel plans.
Do Unit 37 of A2.hr (a lighter one — you've earned it).
Retell your best travel story with clean aspect choices.
Note 5 corrections and add unknown words to the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say your 5 corrected sentences.
Recall the motion-verb family (ići → otići, doći, stići, krenuti).

days.title: Narrate a journey
days.objective: Tell a complete past-tense travel story with controlled aspect.
days.paretoFocus: Narration is the A2 speaking task: sequencing events (perfective) against background (imperfective) with najprije/zatim/potom/kasnije as signposts.
drills: Do Unit 38 of A2.hr.
Outline a real trip: 6 events, 2 background details.
Tell it aloud with najprije, zatim, potom, na kraju.
Tell it again 20% faster without notes.

items: Re-tell the story in 45 seconds.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Justify the aspect of 3 verbs you used.

days.title: Listening: short recorded messages
days.objective: Extract the essentials from voicemails and short announcements.
days.paretoFocus: Understanding short recorded messages is a named A2 can-do and tomorrow's mock-listening warm-up — train the exact skill, not around it.
drills: Do Unit 39 of A2.hr.
Play each unit audio once only and write: who, what, when, where.
Check against the transcript; replay only what you missed.
Shadow the fastest dialogue line by line.

items: Summarise one audio in 2 Croatian sentences.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note your listening weak spot (speed, numbers, names?).

days.title: Mid-phase milestone: Units 1-40 done
days.objective: Finish Unit 40 — the exact halfway point — and verify your progress with a mock listening drill.
days.paretoFocus: Half the e-course, four of eight modules, all core case declensions and the conditional behind you. Measuring now tells you precisely what the second half must fix.
drills: Do Unit 40 of A2.hr — Module 4 complete, Units 1-40 done!
Mock listening drill: 3 unit audios from different modules, one pass each, score your comprehension honestly.
Self-check: declining a full phrase in A, L, D; clitic order; 10 aspect pairs; the conditional.
Write down your 3 weakest areas — they get priority in weeks 15-21.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-listen to the audio you scored worst and close the gap.
Say out loud: halfway done — 40 units to go.

days.title: Odnosi, izgled, budućnost — describing people
days.objective: Describe people's appearance and character in detail.
days.paretoFocus: izgled i karakter vocabulary (visok, plavokos, duhovit, strpljiv) is the raw material for every story that involves a person — which is most stories.
drills: Do Unit 41 of A2.hr.
Learn 15 appearance/character adjectives.
Describe two people you know: 3 sentences of looks, 3 of character.
Mind full adjective agreement in every case you use.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Describe a family member in 4 sentences.
Review your 3 weak areas from the milestone.

days.title: Comparative: stariji, mlađi, ljepši
days.objective: Form comparatives with -iji, -ji and -ši, including the irregulars.
days.paretoFocus: star → stariji, mlad → mlađi, lijep → ljepši, plus irregular dobar → bolji, velik → veći, malen → manji. Comparison powers opinions, shopping and every 'which is better' talk.
drills: Do Unit 42 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · comparative and superlative.
Form comparatives of 10 adjectives across all three suffixes.
Compare 4 pairs of people/places with 'nego' and 'od'.

items: Give the irregulars: dobar, loš, velik, malen.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Compare two cities in 2 sentences.

days.title: Superlative & declining comparisons
days.objective: Form naj- superlatives and decline comparatives like adjectives.
days.paretoFocus: Comparatives decline too ('sa starijim bratom', 'najboljeg prijatelja') — knowing this keeps your case system intact when opinions get superlative.
drills: Do Unit 43 of A2.hr.
Form superlatives: najstariji, najljepši, najbolji.
Decline 'moj najbolji prijatelj' through N, A, D, L.
Answer: Tko je najduhovitija osoba koju znaš? Zašto?

items: Say 'with my older brother' (sa starijim bratom).
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall the three comparative suffixes with one example each.

days.title: Week 15 review + speaking: then vs now
days.objective: Compare your life now with five years ago, live.
days.paretoFocus: 'Then vs now' forces comparatives, past aspect and future plans into one conversation — a complete workout of the phase so far.
drills: 15-minute conversation comparing your life then and now (tutor, partner or spouse).
Use 6 comparatives and 2 superlatives, declined correctly.
Re-do the weakest exercise from Units 41-43.
Note corrections and feed new words to the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say your 3 best comparison sentences.
Recall the irregular comparatives cold.

days.title: Comparing adverbs: brže, bolje, najviše
days.objective: Compare how things are done: adverb comparatives and superlatives.
days.paretoFocus: brzo → brže, dobro → bolje, mnogo → više — adverb comparison completes the comparing toolkit and appears constantly in everyday evaluation.
drills: Do Unit 44 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · comparing adverbs.
Form: brzo/brže/najbrže, dobro/bolje/najbolje, loše/gore/najgore, mnogo/više/najviše.
Make 5 sentences about who does what better/faster/most.

items: Say 'She speaks Croatian better than I do.'
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review adjective superlatives.

days.title: Relationships: veze i prijateljstva
days.objective: Talk about relationships, friendship and family dynamics.
days.paretoFocus: Relationship vocabulary (veza, povjerenje, svađa, podrška) plus opinion frames gives you access to the conversations that actually build friendships.
drills: Do Unit 45 of A2.hr.
Learn 12 relationship words: veza, brak, povjerenje, svađa, pomirenje, podrška…
State 3 opinions: Što je najvažnije u prijateljstvu? (superlatives!)
Describe how you met an important person in your life.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Give your 'most important in friendship' answer cold.
Review adverb comparison.

days.title: Futur II: kad budem imao…
days.objective: Form futur II (budem + participle) and know where it lives.
days.paretoFocus: Futur II never stands alone — it lives inside kad/ako/dok clauses about the future. Learning the form with its habitat prevents years of confusion.
drills: Do Unit 46 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · futur II and time/condition clauses.
Learn budem, budeš, bude, budemo, budete, budu + participle.
Build 5 sentences: Kad budem imao vremena, nazvat ću te.

items: Conjugate budem… cold.
Say 'When I have time (in future), I'll call you.'
Clear your due words in the Words tab.

days.title: Kad, ako, dok + the future
days.objective: Choose correctly between kad (when), ako (if) and dok (while) in future clauses.
days.paretoFocus: Three little conjunctions structure all future planning: certainty (kad), condition (ako), duration (dok). English speakers must unlearn 'will' inside these clauses.
drills: Do Unit 47 of A2.hr.
Contrast: Kad dođeš… / Ako dođeš… / Dok budeš čekao…
Translate 5 English 'when/if I…' sentences without using futur I in the clause.
Write 3 real plans with a kad/ako/dok clause each.

items: Explain kad vs ako in one sentence.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say your 3 real plans.

days.title: Talking about your future
days.objective: Speak for two minutes about your future mixing futur I and futur II.
days.paretoFocus: 'O budućnosti' is where this module's grammar becomes identity: your plans, conditions and hopes in fluent sequence.
drills: Do Unit 48 of A2.hr.
Prepare a 2-minute talk: next year, in five years, kad budeš u mirovini.
Include 2 futur II clauses and 1 conditional wish.
Record it, listen, and fix the worst sentence.

items: Re-deliver the talk without notes.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review kad/ako/dok with one example each.

days.title: Tko radi, ne boji se gladi — the world of work
days.objective: Command core work vocabulary: jobs, workplace, applying.
days.paretoFocus: Work is the number-one adult conversation topic. posao, plaća, radno vrijeme, životopis, razgovor za posao — this lexicon pays rent forever.
drills: Do Unit 49 of A2.hr.
Learn 15 work words: tvrtka, ured, plaća, smjena, kolega, šef, životopis, natječaj…
Describe your job (or dream job): duties, hours, what you like.
Fill in a mock CV-style form in Croatian: name, education, experience.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Describe your workday in 4 sentences.
Review futur II once.

days.title: Week 16 review + native listening
days.objective: Consolidate the comparison-and-future block and try real native media.
days.paretoFocus: You're past Unit 49 — strong enough to start mining HRT news for gist. Native input now compounds daily.
drills: 15-minute conversation: work, future plans, comparisons.
Watch one short HRT news item; get the topic and two facts (visuals help — that's allowed at A2).
Re-do the weakest exercise from Units 44-49.
Note 5 new words from the news for the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Summarise the news item in 2 Croatian sentences.
Recall: futur II paradigm + 3 irregular comparatives.

days.title: Job interview role-play
days.objective: Handle a basic job interview: introduce experience, answer politely.
days.paretoFocus: The interview scenario packs formal register, conditionals, work vocabulary and biography into one high-stakes format. Unit 50 also closes Module 5.
drills: Do Unit 50 of A2.hr — Module 5 complete!
Prepare answers: Recite nam nešto o sebi. Zašto želite ovaj posao?
Role-play the interview aloud, both roles, vi-forms throughout.
Add one polite conditional: Bih li mogao dobiti više informacija o…?

items: Answer 'Recite nam nešto o sebi' in 30 seconds.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note: 50 units down, 30 to go.

days.title: Genitive across the whole phrase (+ čujem)
days.objective: Decline adjectives, demonstratives, possessives and svoj in the genitive; conjugate čuti-type verbs.
days.paretoFocus: The genitive is Croatian's busiest case; extending it across full phrases ('mog novog šefa') completes your declension table. Bonus: čuti → čujem covers piti, čuti and friends.
drills: Do Unit 51 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · adjective declension (genitive).
Decline 6 phrases: taj novi kolega → tog novog kolege.
Conjugate čuti (čujem…) and use it in 3 sentences.

items: Say 'my new boss's office' with genitive.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review interview answers.

days.title: Whose is it? Genitive of possession
days.objective: Use genitiv pripadnosti for ownership and belonging.
days.paretoFocus: 'auto moga brata', 'centar grada' — the possession genitive is how Croatian chains nouns together, replacing a whole English preposition system.
drills: Do Unit 52 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · uses of the genitive.
Build 8 possession chains: vrata učionice, kraj filma, adresa moje sestre.
Decide when a possessive adjective (bratov) beats a genitive (moga brata) — rule of thumb: adjectives for single named owners.

items: Say 'my brother's car' both ways.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review genitive phrase endings.

days.title: Time in the genitive
days.objective: Use vremenski genitiv for when things happen(ed).
days.paretoFocus: prošlog tjedna, svakog jutra, ove godine, jednog dana — the temporal genitive is the default way to timestamp stories, no preposition needed.
drills: Do Unit 53 of A2.hr.
Learn the set: prošlog/ovog/sljedećeg + tjedna/mjeseca; prošle/ove/sljedeće + godine; svakog jutra/dana.
Timestamp 6 sentences about your recent past and near future.
Start a story with 'Jednog dana…' and add 3 sentences.

items: Say 'last week', 'every morning', 'next year' — genitive only.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review genitive of possession.

days.title: Genitive of quantity & description
days.objective: Use the explanatory genitive and quantity genitive fluently.
days.paretoFocus: čaša hladne vode, čovjek dobre volje, puno posla — the genitive after quantities and in descriptions rounds out the case's three big uses.
drills: Do Unit 54 of A2.hr.
Build 6 quantity phrases: šalica jake kave, kilogram svježeg sira, malo slobodnog vremena.
Describe 3 people with objasnidbeni genitiv: žena vedrog duha…
Recap all three genitive uses aloud: time, possession, description/quantity.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Order 'a glass of cold water' correctly.
Name the three genitive uses with one example each.

days.title: Write it: apply for a job
days.objective: Write a short job application and mini-CV in Croatian.
days.paretoFocus: The application letter is formal writing with immediate real-world value — and it audits your genitives, conditionals and vi-forms in one page.
drills: Do Unit 55 of A2.hr.
Write 10 sentences: Javljam se na natječaj… + experience, strengths, availability.
Attach a 6-line CV: education, work history with temporal genitives (prošle godine…).
Read it to your partner and fix what they flag.

items: Read the letter aloud, formal register.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review the genitive uses once more.

days.title: Week 17 speaking day (+ Unit 56)
days.objective: Talk about work and ambitions live, then bank a lighter unit.
days.paretoFocus: Explaining your work in Croatian to a real listener is the strongest retrieval practice this module allows — everything from Unit 49 onward feeds it.
drills: 15-minute conversation: your work, your ideal job, workplace stories.
Do Unit 56 of A2.hr.
Work in 3 temporal genitives and 2 comparatives naturally.
Note corrections; queue unknown words in the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say your 3 best corrected sentences.
Recall: three uses of the genitive.

days.title: Sjećanja — childhood memories
days.objective: Open the memories theme: childhood vocabulary and first recollections.
days.paretoFocus: djetinjstvo, uspomena, sjećati se — memories are the A2 storytelling theme, and everything you know about past aspect finally gets a stage.
drills: Do Unit 57 of A2.hr.
Learn 12 memory words: djetinjstvo, uspomena, sjećati se, odrastati, igračka, susjedstvo…
Answer: Čega se najradije sjećaš iz djetinjstva? (genitive after sjećati se!)
Sketch 5 bullet points of a childhood memory for tomorrow's story.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'I remember my grandmother's garden.'
Review temporal genitives for storytelling.

days.title: Sequencing a story
days.objective: Chain events with najprije, zatim, potom, kasnije, na kraju.
days.paretoFocus: Sequencers plus temporal genitives are the skeleton of narrative — the exact linking skill the A2 writing descriptors name.
drills: Do Unit 58 of A2.hr.
Expand yesterday's bullet points into a sequenced 8-sentence story.
Timestamp it: jednog ljeta, svakog jutra, prošle godine…
Tell it aloud twice; second time from memory.

items: Re-tell the story in 60 seconds.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
List the 5 sequencers cold.

days.title: Kad sam bio dijete…
days.objective: Narrate habitual past (imperfective) against one-time events (perfective).
days.paretoFocus: 'Kad sam bio dijete, svakog ljeta smo išli na more. Jedne godine naučio sam plivati.' — habitual vs punctual is aspect's finest distinction, mastered through memories.
drills: Do Unit 59 of A2.hr.
Write 4 habitual-past sentences (imperfective) and 4 one-time events (perfective).
Merge them into one childhood paragraph.
Note the noun dijete and its odd genitive djeteta — full pattern comes tomorrow.

items: Say one habitual + one one-time memory pair.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review story sequencers.

days.title: Shape-shifting nouns: ime, vrijeme, dijete
days.objective: Decline nouns with -(e)n-/-(e)t- stem extensions.
days.paretoFocus: ime → imena, vrijeme → vremena, dijete → djeteta — a tiny class of very frequent nouns that grows a syllable outside the nominative. Unit 60 closes Module 6.
drills: Do Unit 60 of A2.hr — Module 6 complete!
Study Grammar tab: A2 · special noun patterns (stem extensions).
Decline ime, vrijeme and dijete through the singular.
Use each in a sentence: Nemam vremena. Kako je ime tvog djeteta?

items: Give the genitive of ime, vrijeme, dijete.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note: 60 units down, 20 to go.

days.title: Instrumental across the whole phrase
days.objective: Decline adjectives, demonstratives, possessives and svoj in the instrumental.
days.paretoFocus: 'sa svojom najboljom prijateljicom', 'ovim brzim vlakom' — the instrumental is the last case to get full-phrase treatment. After today, the whole declension table is yours.
drills: Do Unit 61 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · adjective declension (instrumental).
Decline 6 phrases: moja stara prijateljica → s mojom starom prijateljicom.
Write 5 sentences: with whom and by what means you did things last week.

items: Say 'with my best friend' with full agreement.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Celebrate: every case now declines the full phrase.

days.title: Write it: a memory
days.objective: Write a 120-word childhood memory with instrumental company and genitive time.
days.paretoFocus: This text is the memories module in miniature: temporal genitives, aspect contrast, instrumental companions — narrative writing at true A2 level.
drills: Do Unit 62 of A2.hr.
Write ~120 words about one childhood summer or friendship.
Require of yourself: 2 temporal genitives, 2 instrumental phrases, both aspects.
Read it to your partner and fix what they flag.

items: Read the memory aloud with feeling.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review instrumental phrase endings.

days.title: Week 18 review + speaking: a 3-minute memory
days.objective: Tell a complete 3-minute memory story to a live listener.
days.paretoFocus: Swapping childhood stories is intimacy in any language — and if your spouse is Croatian, trading memories over dinner is the best tutor session money can't buy.
drills: Tell your memory story for 3 minutes; then ask for theirs and follow up with questions.
Apply corrections from your posted text.
Re-do the weakest exercise from Units 57-62.
Record the story once more and compare with week 11's recordings — hear the progress.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say 3 corrected sentences.
Recall: ime/vrijeme/dijete genitives + instrumental phrase endings.

days.title: Ime je znak — names & customs
days.objective: Talk about names, nicknames and Croatian naming customs.
days.paretoFocus: imendan, nadimak, prezime — names open culture: why half of Croatia celebrates name days and what surnames in -ić actually mean.
drills: Do Unit 63 of A2.hr.
Learn: ime, prezime, nadimak, imendan, kum/kuma, krštenje, naslijediti ime.
Explain your own name: origin, meaning, who chose it.
Learn 5 common Croatian names and their name-day customs.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'My name means…' or 'I was named after…'.
Decline ime once more (ime, imena, imenu…).

days.title: Declining foreign words: taksi, hobi, intervju
days.objective: Decline foreign nouns ending in -y, -i, -u, -o and -e.
days.paretoFocus: taksi → taksija, hobi → hobija, intervju → intervjua, auto → auta — loanwords flood modern Croatian, and they all obey learnable insertion rules.
drills: Do Unit 64 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · special noun patterns (foreign nouns).
Decline taksi, hobi, intervju and auto through the singular.
Write 4 sentences: Idem taksijem. Nemam hobija…

items: Give the genitive of taksi, hobi, intervju.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review name-day vocabulary.

days.title: radio → radija, Mario → Marija
days.objective: Decline nouns and names in -io with the j-insertion.
days.paretoFocus: The -io pattern (radio, studio, Mario) inserts -j- in every case but the nominative — tiny rule, constant payoff with names and media words.
drills: Do Unit 65 of A2.hr.
Decline radio and Mario: radija, radiju, radijem; Marija, Mariju, s Marijem.
Write 4 sentences mixing -io nouns and yesterday's loanwords.
Say a sentence about a friend named Mario or Dario in three different cases.

items: Say 'on the radio' (na radiju) and 'with Mario' (s Marijem).
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall the foreign-noun genitives from yesterday.

days.title: Present classes: pečem, jedem
days.objective: Conjugate peći-type verbs and the -sti → -dem class.
days.paretoFocus: peći → pečem, moći → mogu/možeš, jesti → jedem, sjesti → sjednem — the kitchen-table verb classes. After this, few present tenses can surprise you.
drills: Do Unit 66 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · more present-tense classes.
Conjugate peći and jesti in full.
Describe cooking a meal: Pečem kruh, jedemo zajedno…

items: Conjugate jesti cold.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review -io declension with one example.

days.title: dajem, prodajem + dio → dijela
days.objective: Conjugate -vati → -jem verbs and decline the noun dio.
days.paretoFocus: davati → dajem, prodavati → prodajem cover give/sell/hand-over verbs; dio → dijela is the last irregular noun on the A2 list. The grammar inventory is now complete.
drills: Do Unit 67 of A2.hr.
Conjugate davati and prodavati in the present.
Decline dio: dijela, dijelu, dijelom; veći dio dana.
Write 4 sentences with dajem/prodajem + a form of dio.

items: Say 'most of the day' (veći dio dana).
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recap: you have now met every A2 grammar structure — list 3 favourites.

days.title: Output: the story of your name
days.objective: Tell and write the story of your name — or a Croatian name you love.
days.paretoFocus: A personal name story deploys the whole Ime je znak module plus declension of real names — memorable content you'll retell at every Croatian table.
drills: Do Unit 68 of A2.hr.
Write 8-10 sentences: your name's origin, who chose it, nicknames, how Croatians pronounce it.
Deliver it as a 90-second talk, twice.
Post the text for corrections and ask how your name declines in Croatian.

items: Re-deliver the talk cold.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Decline your own name (or its Croatian form) through 3 cases.

days.title: Week 19 speaking day (+ Unit 69)
days.objective: Free conversation on names, family history and customs, plus one unit.
days.paretoFocus: Names lead naturally to family history — a conversation that recycles memories, genitives and possessives while feeling nothing like grammar practice.
drills: 15-minute conversation: names and stories in your family (tutor, partner or spouse).
Do Unit 69 of A2.hr.
Ask your partner about their imendan and family names.
Note corrections; queue new words in the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say 3 corrected sentences.
Recall: dajem/prodajem + dio declension.

days.title: Hrvati u svijetu, svijet u Hrvatskoj
days.objective: Open the final theme: diaspora, migration and cultures meeting.
days.paretoFocus: More Croats live abroad than at home — iseljeništvo, domovina, useljenik are the words behind half of Croatian family stories. Unit 70 closes Module 7.
drills: Do Unit 70 of A2.hr — Module 7 complete, one module to go!
Learn 12 words: iseljeništvo, domovina, useljenik, granica, državljanstvo, običaj…
Tell the migration story of a family you know (yours or your spouse's).
Mark on a mental map: where do Croatian communities live worldwide?

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 'My family moved from… to… because…'.
Note: 70 units down, 10 to go.

days.title: Impersonal sentences: Kaže se…
days.objective: Use impersonal constructions: kaže se, može se, treba, hladno mi je.
days.paretoFocus: Impersonals are how Croatian talks about 'people in general' and inner states — 'Ovdje se dobro jede', 'Hladno mi je' — indispensable and utterly unlike English.
drills: Do Unit 71 of A2.hr.
Study Grammar tab: A2 · impersonal sentences.
Build 6 se-impersonals: Kako se kaže…? Ovdje se ne puši. U Hrvatskoj se puno kava pije.
Build 4 dative-state sentences: Hladno mi je. Dosadno joj je. Žao nam je.

items: Say 'How do you say that in Croatian?' impersonally.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review diaspora vocabulary.

days.title: Comparing cultures
days.objective: Compare life in Croatia with life in your country, respectfully and richly.
days.paretoFocus: Culture comparison recycles the whole phase — comparatives, impersonals ('U Hrvatskoj se večera kasnije'), -ski adjectives — in the conversation every foreigner in Croatia gets asked daily.
drills: Do Unit 72 of A2.hr.
Write 6 comparison sentences: food, work hours, coffee culture, family life.
Use 2 impersonals and 3 comparatives among them.
Answer the classic: Što ti se najviše sviđa u Hrvatskoj?

items: Give your 'best thing about Croatia' answer cold.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review impersonal patterns.

days.title: Read the news (simple items)
days.objective: Read short news items and extract names, numbers and the gist.
days.paretoFocus: Reading simple news with numbers and names is a named A2 can-do — and HRT's short items are the perfect daily habit to carry into B1.
drills: Do Unit 73 of A2.hr.
Read 3 short HRT news items; for each note: who, what, where, when, how many.
Guess 5 unknown words from context before looking them up.
Retell the most interesting item in 3 Croatian sentences.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-tell one news item cold.
Review culture-comparison sentences.

days.title: Watch the news for gist
days.objective: Follow TV news topic-by-topic using visuals as support.
days.paretoFocus: Getting the gist of TV news where visuals support the commentary is the A2 listening ceiling — hit it now and B1 media becomes a step, not a wall.
drills: Do Unit 74 of A2.hr.
Watch 10 minutes of HRT Dnevnik; write one line per story: topic + one fact.
Rewatch one story and count how much more you catch.
Shadow the anchor for 30 seconds — pure pronunciation gold.

items: Summarise 2 stories in Croatian.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note your news comprehension percentage — you'll re-test at B1.

days.title: Write it: a letter abroad
days.objective: Write a warm personal letter to a friend or relative in another country.
days.paretoFocus: The personal letter — greetings, thanks, news, questions, linked with i/ali/jer and najprije/zatim/kasnije — is the A2 writing descriptor made real.
drills: Do Unit 75 of A2.hr.
Write ~130 words: Draga…/Dragi…, your news, one memory, questions for them, warm closing.
Check your connectors: at least i, ali, jer plus two sequencers.
Post it for corrections — this is your last corrected text of the phase.

items: Read the letter aloud.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Review letter openings and closings (Draga…, Srdačan pozdrav…).

days.title: Week 20 review + speaking
days.objective: Consolidate the world-and-Croatia theme in a long conversation.
days.paretoFocus: With 75 units behind you, today's conversation should feel qualitatively different from week 9's — measure that difference and mine what still creaks.
drills: 20-minute conversation: Croatia vs the world — food, customs, news of the week.
Apply corrections from your posted letter.
Re-do the weakest exercise from Units 70-75.
List the 5 grammar points you still hesitate on — next week's sweeps will target them.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-say 3 corrected sentences.
Recall impersonals + one culture comparison.

days.title: Grammar sweep 1: the full case system
days.objective: Decline any full phrase in any case, on demand.
days.paretoFocus: One timed sweep through the complete declension system — nouns, adjectives, pronouns, svoj, foreign nouns, ime/dijete/dio — turns twelve weeks of pieces into one machine.
drills: Do Unit 76 of A2.hr.
Decline 5 mixed phrases through all 7 cases (include one foreign noun and one stem-extension noun).
Speed round: 20 random case prompts, answer in under 5 seconds each.
Re-study the Grammar tab topic where you were slowest.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Decline 'moj najbolji prijatelj' through all cases, aloud.
Give the genitive of: taksi, ime, dijete, dio.

days.title: Grammar sweep 2: clitics & aspect
days.objective: Produce correct clitic chains and aspect choices at conversation speed.
days.paretoFocus: Clitic order and aspect are the two A2 systems that most resist conscious control — a dedicated speed day pushes them toward automaticity.
drills: Do Unit 77 of A2.hr.
Rapid-fire: 10 sentences with li + dative + accusative clitics, spoken not written.
Aspect gauntlet: 15 prompts, pick and say the right verb of the pair instantly.
Tell one 60-second story with deliberate background/event aspect contrast.

items: Recite the clitic chain and give one maximal example.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Give both aspects of 8 pairs, fast.

days.title: Grammar sweep 3: conditional, futur II, comparison
days.objective: Command the mood-and-comparison block without hesitation.
days.paretoFocus: The conditional, futur II with kad/ako/dok, and comparatives are your expressive upgrades this phase — the final sweep makes sure they survive contact with real conversation.
drills: Do Unit 78 of A2.hr.
Produce: 4 conditionals (one per meaning), 3 futur II clauses, 5 declined comparatives.
Translate 8 mixed English prompts ('I would…', 'when I get…', 'better than…') at speed.
One 90-second talk: 'Moj život za pet godina' using all three systems.

items: Say a kad-bih wish and a kad-budem plan back to back.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall the irregular comparatives one last time.

days.title: Dress rehearsal: four skills
days.objective: Simulate the A2 exam experience across listening, reading, writing and speaking.
days.paretoFocus: A timed four-skill rehearsal two days before the milestone finds the last soft spots while there's still time to patch them.
drills: Do Unit 79 of A2.hr.
Listening: 2 unit audios, one pass, questions answered.
Reading + writing: one Razgovarajte s nama! text, then a 100-word response, timed 20 minutes.
Speaking: 3-minute monologue on a surprise topic (pick blind from your theme list).

items: Review every error from today honestly.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Pick tomorrow's talk topic: your Croatian story so far.

days.title: Unit 80 — the e-course is done!
days.objective: Finish the final unit of E-tečaj A2.hr and deliver a 5-minute talk.
days.paretoFocus: Eighty units, eight modules, ninety days. Closing the course with a long free talk proves the knowledge left the screen and moved into you.
drills: Do Unit 80 of A2.hr — Module 8 complete, ALL 80 UNITS DONE!
Deliver and record a 5-minute talk: your life, your plans, your Croatian journey.
Watch tonight's HRT news and note how much you now follow.
Write one sentence to your day-61 self — in Croatian, obviously.

items: Listen to your talk and mark 3 wins and 3 fixes.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Prepare your can-do checklist for tomorrow's milestone.

days.title: A2 milestone: can-dos, quiz and the B1 plan
days.objective: Confirm A2 against the official can-dos, pass the A2 quiz, and lay out your B1 phase.
days.paretoFocus: A milestone measured honestly is worth a month of drifting study. You can now shop, arrange, apologize, narrate and describe — certify it, celebrate it, and aim the habit at B1.
drills: Self-assess against the A2 can-dos: everyday transactions, arranging meetings, describing people/home/work/past events, short letters and messages.
Take the A2 quiz in the app and complete any open A2 Feynman concepts.
Have a celebratory 20-minute conversation — tutor or your Croatian spouse, who has heard every step of this.
Plan the B1 phase: Razgovarajte s nama! B1, daily HRT news, weekly conversation sessions with your partner, and the B1 grammar ladder in the app.

items: Tick off every A2 can-do statement in writing.
Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Celebrate — 150 days, A2 reached, B1 in sight!
